support_note: Piece Picking variant is the reference baseline in the strategy selection scenario — the simplest case against which all batch/zone/wave/automation strategies are compared.
Batch Picking variant is one of the primary strategies evaluated in strategy selection.
Batch Picking variant is the canonical setting for batch size optimization — its configurable batch_size parameter is the central decision variable.
Zone Picking variant is one of the primary strategies evaluated in strategy selection.
Zone Picking variant is the canonical setting for picker staffing and zone allocation scenarios — its zone-based work allocation is exactly what the scenario optimizes.
Wave Picking variant is one of the primary strategies evaluated in strategy selection. DHL used this strategy in the AnyLogic case study with quantified results.
Wave Picking variant supports the staffing scenario with both zone and wave-timing as controllable parameters.
Wave Picking variant can serve as the 'advanced manual' baseline in the automation investment decision, representing the best non-automated alternative before considering goods-to-person.
Goods-to-Person variant can be included in strategy selection comparisons but is most relevant when automation is a candidate strategy rather than a refinement of manual picking.
Goods-to-Person variant is the canonical setting for the automation investment decision — it represents the 'automated' alternative being compared against manual picking variants.
